title: author-agent-spec validation is a false positive for misclassified prompts
description: author-agent-spec validates specs by round-tripping through parseAgentYaml. When a prompt is misclassified as a template path (FB-001), parseAgentYaml succeeds — it stores the value in taskPromptTemplate instead of taskPrompt. The validation sees a valid AgentSpec and passes. The failure only surfaces later in compileAgent when it tries to open the prompt text as a file. This means author-agent-spec reports success for specs that will fail at dispatch.
actual_behavior: Spec with 'Read docs/03-solar-engine.md' as inline task prompt passes author-agent-spec validation. call-agent fails with ENOENT trying to open the prompt as a file.
expected_behavior: Validation should catch that the prompt is misclassified, or the dispatcher should handle missing template files gracefully (fall back to inline interpretation when template file doesn't exist).
reproduction: Same as FB-001. author-agent-spec succeeds, call-agent fails.
